title: Level 4.3
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/inter-tight'); }} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 4.3: "The Cubicles"}}{{Background|file=Cubicles.webp}} {{Headerbox|file=Cubiclegif.png|header=All work and no play.}}
{{Class 2|class= 2|safety=Unsafe|security=Stable|entity=Low Entity Count|hexagon=2|color-h=64|color-s=19%|color-l=48%}}
==Description==
'''Level 4.3''' is an endless office filled with cubicles, with several hallways connecting different areas together. These cubicles contain black office chairs, pencils, pencil sharpeners, erasers, tables, paper, and computers (though not all of them are functional). Food and drinks can be found in refrigerators (including '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]'''), making '''Level 4.3''' ideal for finding resources. In certain areas of '''Level 4.3''', a multitude of load-bearing walls can be found grouped closely together. These walls can be broken, but breaking them does not appear to have any effect on the Level's structural integrity as a whole. A white fog also subsists within this level at all times, the source of which is unknown. The sounds of papers shuffling, pencils writing, and occasional (albeit unintelligible) human voices can be heard throughout the level, as well as the muffled sound of rain and occasional thunder, the source of which is presumed to be from '''[[Level 4]]'''. Entities can sparsely be found in this sub-level, the most common of which being '''[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]''', often found pretending to be working, waiting for unfortunate wanderers to pass by. Adult '''[[Entity 9|Facelings]]''' can be found in the level and will happily escort you to the nearest exit as long as you speak to them politely. Child '''Facelings''' are never present in '''Level 4.3'''. Several separate areas and rooms can be found within '''Level 4.3''', such as meeting rooms, lavatories, and the hallways that can be used to enter and exit this level from, the managers' offices. The whole of '''Level 4.3''' is unnaturally humid, causing a high amount of mold to grow. As well, '''[[Object 10|Backshrooms]]''', '''Cinnamon Shrooms''' and '''Window Shrooms''' appear to thrive here, especially in hallways. Several wanderers have confirmed to have seen high amounts of vegetation further into the level. The sight of vegetation growing inside a series of office cubicles naturally causes an eerie and liminal feeling. Whether this is affectatious to wanderers or not is still undetermined. '''Level 4.3''''s class has been of great dispute due to the heavy psychological effects caused by this sub-level. Any and all wanderers present in '''Level 4.3''' will have an increase in work ethic, drive, energy, and morale. This feeling can last even after the wanderer has left '''Level 4.3''' for up to 48 hours. Wanderers may also begin to feel the desire to provide assistance to any other wanderer they encounter with anything they may need. This effect can cause wanderers to wish to stay and to "Work" within '''Level 4.3''' and to reside here permanently. The further one wanders within '''Level 4.3''', the more abandoned and decrepit it becomes, with more mold, lichen, and moss seen growing on the cubicles, roofs, floors, and walls, as well as an increase in '''Backshrooms'''. '''Almond Water''' will begin to become more scarce, and entity encounters will increase. Due to this, wandering too far within '''Level 4.3''' is highly advised against. The furthest documented travel of a wanderer into '''Level 4.3''' is around 300 miles, whereupon the wanderer fell through the floor and was taken directly to [[Level 5.3]]. The wanderer reports to have seen vast amounts of lush, leafy vegetation, with almost every cubicle being destroyed.
==Gallery== File:Cubicles.webp File:Level4.3.jpg File:Cubiclegif.png File:EndlessOffice.jpeg
There are several laptops found within '''Level 4.3''', almost all of which are uncharged and without a battery. A very small amount of computers have information possibly providing evidence pertaining to the creation and origin of '''Level 4.3'''. Emails and documents can be found within these laptops, though as of '''{{#time: F d, Y }}''', only one email has been archived. ''Archived on '''March 3, 2020'''.''
'''>ARCHIVED EMAIL<'''
==Archived Email: "To Whom It May Concern"== {{Color|background color=White|border color=Gray|text color=Black|text=
To whom it may concern, Any loss of equipment will be taken directly out of your paycheck. Several computers and other essential devices given to employees have gone missing due to sheer incompetence. Repeated loss of assets ''will'' result in termination. This will take effect immediately. This is non-negotiable. Any excuses, including "it fell through the floor", which I am fed up with hearing, will be disregarded. If you have any questions you can email me at ''kylestewart@gmail.com''.
}}
==Entities== Several entities can be found within '''Level 4.3''' in small amounts. These entities include: |'''[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]]'''| |'''[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]'''| |'''[[Entity 5|Clumps]]'''| |'''[[Entity 9|Facelings]]'''| |'''[[Entity 2|Windows]]'''|
==Colonies and Outposts== ===T.A.L. (The Analysts of '''Level 4.3''')=== Dedicated to discovering and archiving any documents, emails, or notes within the computers of '''Level 4.3'''. Friendly to wanderers and will to trade. Will accept any information from computers in exchange for resources such as '''Almond Water.''' Any attempt to destroy any one of the computers will result in violent retaliation. === Backroom Colonists === Being close to level 4, considered to be a nexus to a multitude of levels, the Colonists decided to commit resources to allow easy transfer between Level 4 and 5, and to safeguard the main base if attacked, as well as help transport resources. Friendly to wanderers and groups like T.A.L, usually helping them find archives.
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Walking down a dim white series of hallways in '''[[Level 4]]''' can lead you here. *Finding a pure white door in '''[[Level 1]]''' will lead here. * Finding a tunnel in [[Level 3.6]] can lead here. *Did you know that certain staircases in [[Level 47]] may lead here? ===Exits=== *No-clipping through the floor will lead to either '''[[Level 4]]''' or '''[[Level 5]]'''. *Accidental noclipping will take one to [[Level 11.3]]. *Walking down a dim white series of hallways can lead to '''[[Level 4]]'''. *Entering a turquoise green door sends wanderers to '''[[Level 597]]'''. *Punching a manager office door will lead to '''[[Level 11]]'''. *Walking deep into the sub-level and then no-clipping through the floor will lead to '''[[Level 5.3]]'''. *Entering a red door will send one to [[Level 410]]. *Typing "0000" into the command console of a computer will take you to '''[[Level 0]]'''. *Typing "0009" into the command console of a computer will take you to '''[[Level 9]]'''. *Typing "0011" into the command console of a computer will take you to '''[[Level 11]]'''. It is worth noting that the last method of egress rarely works for other levels. You cannot type "0399" to be transported to '''[[Level 399]]''', for instance.
